How Long Does It Take to Register a Trademark in China?

# How Long Does It Take to Register a Trademark in China?

Published: June 8, 2026 | Category: Guide | Read Time: 12 min | Tags: trademark-timeline, registration-process, CNIPA, China-trademark

📋 Key Takeaway

The typical timeline for registering a trademark in China is 10-14 months, but this can vary significantly based on factors like trademark complexity, CNIPA workload, and whether objections or oppositions occur. Understanding the process helps you plan ahead and avoid costly delays.

⏱️ The Complete Trademark Registration Timeline

Phase 1: Application Preparation (1-2 weeks)

What Happens:

  • ✓Trademark search and analysis
  • ✓Document preparation
  • ✓Classification selection
  • ✓Application drafting

RTMCN's Process:

  • ✓Comprehensive search across CNIPA database
  • ✓Risk assessment and recommendations
  • ✓Document review and preparation
  • ✓Application submission

Time Factors:

  • ✓Simple trademarks: 1 week
  • ✓Complex designs or multiple classes: 2 weeks
  • ✓Need for Chinese translation: Additional 3-5 days

Phase 2: Formal Examination (1-3 months)

What Happens:

  • ✓CNIPA reviews application for completeness
  • ✓Checks for obvious conflicts
  • ✓Verifies documentation
  • ✓Assigns application number

Examination Criteria:

  • ✓Application form completeness
  • ✓Required documents attached
  • ✓Correct classification
  • ✓Proper trademark specimen
  • ✓Fee payment confirmed

Time Factors:

  • ✓Simple applications: 1 month
  • ✓Complex cases: 2-3 months
  • ✓Backlog periods: May take longer

What Can Go Wrong:

  • ✓Incomplete documentation
  • ✓Incorrect classification
  • ✓Missing information
  • ✓Fee issues

Phase 3: Substantive Examination (6-9 months)

What Happens:

  • ✓Detailed review of trademark
  • ✓Comparison with existing marks
  • ✓Assessment of distinctiveness
  • ✓Check for prohibited elements

Examination Criteria:

  • ✓Distinctiveness: Is the mark unique enough?
  • ✓Similarity: Does it conflict with existing marks?
  • ✓Legality: Does it contain prohibited elements?
  • ✓Classification: Is it correctly categorized?

Time Factors:

  • ✓Simple, distinctive marks: 6 months
  • ✓Common or descriptive marks: 8-9 months
  • ✓High-volume periods: May take longer

Common Issues:

  • ✓Similar to existing marks
  • ✓Lacks distinctiveness
  • ✓Contains prohibited elements
  • ✓Incorrect classification

Phase 4: Publication for Opposition (3 months)

What Happens:

  • ✓Trademark published in Trademark Gazette
  • ✓3-month opposition period begins
  • ✓Third parties can file objections
  • ✓No extensions possible

What This Means:

  • ✓Your mark is publicly visible
  • ✓Competitors can oppose your registration
  • ✓Squatters may try to block you
  • ✓Strategic timing is important

Time Factors:

  • ✓Fixed 3-month period
  • ✓No extensions
  • ✓Opposition may delay registration

Phase 5: Registration Certificate (1-2 months)

What Happens:

  • ✓If no opposition filed
  • ✓CNIPA issues registration certificate
  • ✓Trademark officially registered
  • ✓Protection begins

What You Receive:

  • ✓Registration certificate
  • ✓Official registration number
  • ✓Protection for 10 years
  • ✓Right to use ® symbol

Time Factors:

  • ✓No opposition: 1 month
  • ✓With opposition: Additional 6-12 months

⏱️ Factors That Affect Timeline

1. Trademark Complexity

Simple Trademarks (Faster):

  • ✓Single word or number
  • ✓Distinctive design
  • ✓No Chinese characters
  • ✓Single class

Complex Trademarks (Slower):

  • ✓Common words or phrases
  • ✓Descriptive terms
  • ✓Chinese characters
  • ✓Multiple classes

Example:

  • ✓"RTMCN" (simple, distinctive): 10 months
  • ✓"CHINA TRADEMARK SERVICES" (descriptive): 14 months

2. CNIPA Workload

Peak Periods (Slower):

  • ✓January-March (post-holiday backlog)
  • ✓September-November (year-end rush)
  • ✓Major policy changes

Faster Periods:

  • ✓April-August
  • ✓December
  • ✓After policy updates

Current Status (2026):

  • ✓Average processing time: 10-12 months
  • ✓Backlog: Moderate
  • ✓Efficiency improvements ongoing

3. Classification Strategy

Single Class (Faster):

  • ✓One application, one class
  • ✓Simpler examination
  • ✓Lower cost
  • ✓Faster processing

Multiple Classes (Slower):

  • ✓Multiple applications needed
  • ✓Each examined separately
  • ✓Higher cost
  • ✓Longer total time

Strategy Tip:

  • ✓File all related classes together
  • ✓Use RTMCN's expertise to optimize classification
  • ✓Consider future expansion needs

4. Objections and Oppositions

No Objections (Faster):

  • ✓Smooth examination
  • ✓No delays
  • ✓Standard timeline

With Objections (Slower):

  • ✓CNIPA may raise concerns
  • ✓Requires response
  • ✓Additional 2-3 months
  • ✓May need amendments

With Opposition (Slowest):

  • ✓Third party files opposition
  • ✓Requires defense
  • ✓Additional 6-12 months
  • ✓May need legal action

📋 How to Speed Up the Process

1. Professional Preparation

Why It Helps:

  • ✓Complete documentation first time
  • ✓Correct classification from start
  • ✓Avoidable delays eliminated
  • ✓Expert guidance throughout

RTMCN's Advantage:

  • ✓Licensed CNIPA agent
  • ✓100% success rate
  • ✓Local expertise
  • ✓Bilingual support

2. Strategic Filing

Best Practices:

  • ✓File early (1-2 years before market entry)
  • ✓Choose distinctive marks
  • ✓Avoid common/descriptive terms
  • ✓Consider Chinese translations

RTMCN's Approach:

  • ✓Comprehensive trademark search
  • ✓Risk assessment
  • ✓Strategic recommendations
  • ✓Proactive protection

3. Response Speed

Why It Matters:

  • ✓CNIPA sets deadlines
  • ✓Late responses cause delays
  • ✓Quick action prevents issues
  • ✓Professional handling helps

RTMCN's Service:

  • ✓Dedicated agent assigned
  • ✓Deadline monitoring
  • ✓Prompt responses
  • ✓Regular updates

4. Avoid Common Mistakes

Mistakes That Cause Delays:

  • ✓Incomplete applications
  • ✓Wrong classification
  • ✓Poor quality specimens
  • ✓Missing documents
  • ✓Late responses

RTMCN's Prevention:

  • ✓Thorough review process
  • ✓Quality control
  • ✓Deadline management
  • ✓Expert oversight

📌 What Happens If There Are Problems?

CNIPA Objections

Common Objections:

  • ✓Similar to existing mark
  • ✓Lacks distinctiveness
  • ✓Contains prohibited elements
  • ✓Incorrect classification

How RTMCN Handles:

  • ✓Analyzes objection grounds
  • ✓Prepares strong response
  • ✓Provides evidence
  • ✓Submits on time

Timeline Impact: +2-3 months

Third-Party Opposition

Who Can Oppose:

  • ✓Owners of similar marks
  • ✓Competitors
  • ✓Trademark squatters
  • ✓Industry associations

RTMCN's Defense:

  • ✓Evidence of prior rights
  • ✓Market analysis
  • ✓Legal arguments
  • ✓Negotiation if needed

Timeline Impact: +6-12 months

Appeals and Reviews

If Initial Decision Unfavorable:

  • ✓Appeal to Trademark Review Board
  • ✓Further appeal to court
  • ✓Additional evidence submission
  • ✓Extended timeline

RTMCN's Support:

  • ✓Legal representation
  • ✓Evidence gathering
  • ✓Strategic planning
  • ✓Court appearances

Timeline Impact: +12-24 months

⏱️ Real-World Timeline Examples

Example 1: Simple Trademark (RTMCN)

Timeline:

  • ✓Application: 1 week
  • ✓Formal Exam: 1 month
  • ✓Substantive Exam: 6 months
  • ✓Publication: 3 months
  • ✓Certificate: 1 month
  • ✓Total: 11 months

Success Factors:

  • ✓Distinctive mark
  • ✓Professional preparation
  • ✓No objections
  • ✓Smooth process

Example 2: Complex Trademark (Multiple Classes)

Timeline:

  • ✓Application: 2 weeks
  • ✓Formal Exam: 2 months
  • ✓Substantive Exam: 8 months
  • ✓Publication: 3 months
  • ✓Certificate: 1 month
  • ✓Total: 14 months

Challenges:

  • ✓Multiple classes
  • ✓Descriptive elements
  • ✓Thorough examination
  • ✓Higher scrutiny

Example 3: Trademark with Opposition

Timeline:

  • ✓Application: 1 week
  • ✓Formal Exam: 1 month
  • ✓Substantive Exam: 7 months
  • ✓Publication: 3 months
  • ✓Opposition Defense: 8 months
  • ✓Certificate: 1 month
  • ✓Total: 20 months

Resolution:

  • ✓Strong defense filed
  • ✓Evidence provided
  • ✓Negotiation successful
  • ✓Registration secured

✅ RTMCN's Average Processing Time

Current Statistics (2026)

Average Time: 10-14 months

Success Rate: 100%

Client Satisfaction: 98%

Breakdown:

  • ✓Simple trademarks: 10-11 months
  • ✓Complex trademarks: 12-14 months
  • ✓With objections: 14-18 months
  • ✓With opposition: 18-24 months

Expertise:

  • ✓Licensed CNIPA agent
  • ✓Deep process knowledge
  • ✓Strong relationships

Service:

📌 Planning Your Trademark Strategy

When to File

Ideal Timing:

  • ✓1-2 years before market entry
  • ✓Before product launch
  • ✓Before marketing campaigns
  • ✓Before partnerships

Why Early Filing Matters:

  • ✓Prevents squatting
  • ✓Establishes priority
  • ✓Allows time for issues
  • ✓Reduces stress

Budget Planning

Costs to Consider:

  • ✓Application fees
  • ✓Agent fees
  • ✓Translation costs
  • ✓Opposition defense (if needed)

RTMCN's Pricing:

  • ✓Transparent fees
  • ✓No hidden costs
  • ✓Package options
  • ✓Payment plans available

Risk Assessment

Factors to Evaluate:

  • ✓Trademark distinctiveness
  • ✓Industry competition
  • ✓Squatting risk
  • ✓Market timing

RTMCN's Analysis:

  • ✓Comprehensive search
  • ✓Risk report
  • ✓Strategic recommendations
  • ✓Ongoing monitoring
